synthesis.summary: CMCSA dropped ~13% on 3x normal volume, a significant single-day move with no supplied news to explain it. The magnitude and unusual volume suggest a discrete catalyst — likely an earnings miss, guidance cut, or major business update — but no news items were provided to confirm the mechanism.

QCOM surged 11.15% on 3.24x normal volume with no supplied news to explain the move. The magnitude and unusual volume suggest a specific catalyst — likely an earnings beat, guidance raise, licensing deal, or options-driven squeeze — but none of that is present in the input data to confirm.

ARM is up ~15% today with no supplied news to explain the move. The 5-day gain of ~41%, elevated relative volume (2x normal), and an RSI of 87 suggest sustained momentum buying, but without a news catalyst in the provided set, this could reflect options flow, short covering, or sector rotation that isn't visible here.

AAOI surged 17.67% today with no supplied news to explain the move. The relatively modest relative volume (1.16x) is unusual for a move of this magnitude, suggesting the catalyst may be options flow, short squeeze activity, or sector rotation rather than a public fundamental announcement.

CAR (Avis Budget Group) is down 10.7% today and has lost nearly 59% over the past five days, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The magnitude and multi-day trend suggest a significant catalyst — possibly an earnings miss, guidance cut, or macro pressure on rental car demand — but nothing in the available news can be cited. Volume is only marginally elevated (rel_volume 1.1), which is unusual for a move of this size and may point to continued selling pressure from a prior catalyst rather than fresh news today.

CC is up ~10% today with elevated volume and an RSI of 77, signaling an overbought condition, but no news has been supplied to explain the move. The 5-day gain of nearly 15% suggests this is part of a multi-day run, possibly driven by options flow, sector rotation, or short covering — none of which are visible in the available data.

EOSE is up 10.55% on 1.41x relative volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The gain could reflect options flow, short-squeeze dynamics, or sector rotation in energy storage names, but none of that is substantiated by available information.

MXL is up 76% today on volume 9x its average, with RSI at 97 and a 5-day gain of 129% — an extraordinary move by any measure. No news items were supplied, so the catalyst cannot be identified from available information; this could reflect an earnings surprise, M&A speculation, options gamma squeeze, or short covering, but none of that can be confirmed.

OGN surged 31% on 4.25x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. This type of sharp, high-volume spike without a visible catalyst is consistent with short squeeze dynamics, options flow, or undisclosed corporate event (e.g., M&A rumor), none of which can be confirmed from available information.

PD is up ~16% on 3x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. This could reflect options activity, short covering, or sector rotation, but none of those mechanisms are visible in the available data.

POET is up 29% today and 108% over five days on nearly 6x normal volume, but no news has been supplied to explain the move. The overbought RSI (81) and extreme short-term momentum suggest possible options flow, short squeeze dynamics, or retail-driven speculation — none of which can be confirmed from available information.

RMBS is up 14.5% today with volume more than double its average and RSI deep in overbought territory, but no news has been supplied to explain the move. This could reflect earnings, a contract win, or options/institutional flow — none of which are in the available data.

SKYQ dropped nearly 15% today and is down over 24% in the past five days, but no news in the supplied feed explains the move. Notably, relative volume is extremely low (0.07), suggesting thin trading conditions may be amplifying price swings rather than a broad selloff driven by a specific catalyst.

STNE surged 23% on 1.74x relative volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The spike could reflect options flow, short covering (STNE has historically carried elevated short interest), or sector rotation into Latin American fintech, but none of these can be confirmed from available data.

YSS dropped 13.27% today on volume 1.6x the norm, but no news items were supplied that explain the selloff. The move could stem from sector rotation, options flow, or an event not captured in the available news feed.

INTC surged ~24% on elevated volume with no news items supplied to explain the move. A gain of this magnitude likely reflects a major catalyst — such as an M&A report, earnings surprise, or strategic announcement — but none of that information is present in the input. This move cannot be attributed to any identifiable news mechanism from available data.

KO is up 0.41% on below-average volume (rel. volume 0.86) with no news catalyst in the input set. The mild gain and bullish MACD cross are consistent with routine price drift or sector rotation rather than a company-specific event.

NVDA surged 4.26% on elevated volume despite news that DeepSeek built its V4 model on Huawei chips rather than Nvidia hardware — a development that would normally pressure the stock. The supplied news is bearish for Nvidia's China market share, not bullish, so it does not explain the upward move. The rally is more likely driven by broader market flows, options activity, or factors not captured in the provided news.

RKLB dropped ~5.9% on above-average volume with no identifiable news catalyst in the supplied feed. The move could reflect profit-taking after a strong prior run, broader sector rotation out of small-cap aerospace/space names, or options/institutional flow not visible here.

XOM is down 1.14% today with no specific news to explain the move. Volume is notably light at 48% of average and RSI sits at 41.9, suggesting mild selling pressure rather than a news-driven event — likely attributable to broader energy sector rotation or oil price softness rather than any company-specific catalyst.
